free_text: Production of longer lived radionuclides in deuteron
induced reactions on niobium
One irradiation.
CGR-560 cyclotron. Two irradiations.
High purity niobium foils, thickness: 8-10 micron.
Primary deuteron energies: 40 MeV, 20 MeV, 20 MeV,
beam intensities: 100-200 nA,
irradiation time: 30-60 min,
gamma spectrometric measurements started: about 5 hours
after the end of the irradiations.
The stacks were irradiated in a short Faraday-cup
target holder. Two or three measurements were made on
each Nb sample while the monitor foils were measured
once. Non-destructive measurement without chemical
separation.
The determination of the average intensity of the
bombarding beam and the energy degradation along the
stack are based on the simultaneously measured monitor
reactions and comparison with recommended values.
Al: 100 micron, Ti: 20 micron, Fe: 14.5 micron.
Chapter 4
The energy degradation was initially determined by
calculation using the formulas and tables of Andersen
and Ziegler.
The uncertainties of the mean deuteron
energies in the individual foils were estimated from
* the energy uncertainty of the primary beam,
* the uncertainty of the thickness and uniformity of
the foils in the irradiated stacks,
* energy straggling and
* increasing energy spread.
The uncertainties on the energy of the front foil of
a stack are around 0.3 MeV and increase up to
1.2-2.7 MeV for the last foils of the stack.
The estimated total uncertainty on the cross
sections contains uncertainties of the following
linearly contributing sources:
* peak areas,
* efficiency,
* gamma abundances,
* number of target nuclei,
* beam intensity
and amount to about 12%. Nonlinear contributions like
waiting or measuring time, half-life are not
considered.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
The measured experimental data were compared to
and analyzed with theoretical calculations based on
the code ALICE-IPPE.
On the basis of the new experimental cross
sections integral yields were calculated.
S. Takacs, 2007.
KB
reaction code was corrected.
